chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C.P.No.D-441 of 2023 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections at flag "A". 2. For hearing of main case. ------ 22.04.2025 Mr. Ghulam Rasool M. Narejo, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Liaquat Ali Shar, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. Mr. Ashfaque Hussain Abro, Advocate for Respondents No.1 to 3. Mr. Habibullah G. Ghouri, Advocate for Respondents No.5 to 7. ------ By virtue of this petition, the petitioner has prayed for following relief(s): (a). That this Honourable Court may be pleased to direct respondents No.1 to 4 to provide latest fresh schedule of cattle market/parri of Taluka Kandhkot and Tangwani on different cattle’s. (b). That this honourable court may be pleased to direct respondents No.5, 6 & 7 not to charge taxes in excess without due course of law and to provide valid receipt with stamp while showing tax on it. (c). An other relief as deems fit may be awarded to the petitioner. At the very outset, learned counsels for the respondents submit that a Gazette notification alongwith copy of Circular dated 26.06.2024 was issued by the competent authority and copy of same was submitted before this Court on the last date, hence handed over copy to learned counsel for the petitioner in Court today; they, therefore, submit that purpose of the petition in view of the gazette notification has been served, hence seek its disposal. Learned counsel for petitioner submits that gazette notification together with circular has been handed over to him by counsel for respondents in Court today covers the prayer clause (a) while prayer clause (b) is yet to be complied with / acceded to.

At the very outset, learned counsels for the respondents submit that a Gazette notification alongwith copy of Circular dated 26.06.2024 was issued by the competent authority and copy of same was submitted before this Court on the last date, hence handed over copy to learned counsel for the petitioner in Court today; they, therefore, submit that purpose of the petition in view of the gazette notification has been served, hence seek its disposal. Learned counsel for petitioner submits that gazette notification together with circular has been handed over to him by counsel for respondents in Court today covers the prayer clause (a) while prayer clause (b) is yet to be complied with / acceded to. Counsel for respondents submit that at the moment entire system is without contract and now they are going to announce a schedule for awarding contract of the same to any reasonable person against a bid then a newly contractor may collect the taxes as per schedule under captioned gazette notification. Learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh has also no objection for disposal of the petition. Learned counsel for the petitioner in view of above joint statement of other side has no objection. Accordingly and in view of the above, instant petition is hereby disposed of. Judge Judge Manzoor
